evals.expected_output: Read the design and project context, evaluate against the 7-dimension rubric, and write review.md next to design.md with a Pass verdict and Go for spec-plan. No fabricated blockers; findings minimal (Minor at most).
expectations: A review.md is written to the same directory as the reviewed design.md (fixtures/clean/)
The verdict is Pass and spec-plan readiness is Go
There are zero Blocker and zero Major findings; the review does not invent blockers to look thorough
All 7 dimensions appear in the Dimension Summary, each marked sound or with only Minor findings
The original design.md is left unmodified

evals.expected_output: Detect missing load-bearing sections (Error Handling, Testing), the empty/placeholder Decision Record, the missing Open Questions section, and the in-app notification goal with no design support. Assign Completeness Blockers and a Reject verdict / No-Go.
expectations: The verdict is Reject and spec-plan readiness is No-Go
At least one Blocker is raised for the missing Error Handling and/or Testing sections (D1 Completeness)
The empty/placeholder Decision Record ('TBD' / 'To be decided') is flagged (D1 or D3)
The in-app notification goal having no corresponding design element is flagged (D1 Completeness)
Every finding cites a concrete location in design.md and gives a recommendation

evals.expected_output: Recognize the design is structurally complete but the generic PreferenceEngine (plugin registry, multiple storage backends, cache, event bus, audit subscriber) is unjustified by the Goals and contradicts the Non-Goals. Raise D6 Over-Engineering findings and recommend the simpler single-column approach the doc itself listed. Verdict Revise.
expectations: The review's dominant findings are Over-Engineering (D6), not Completeness — it acknowledges the doc has all sections
It identifies specific unjustified machinery (e.g., multiple storage backends, cache, event bus, plugin registry) as beyond the stated Goals
It points out the design contradicts the Non-Goals (single value, theme only, no custom themes) and that the Decision Record justifies the framework with speculative future needs
It recommends the simpler 'single column users.theme' alternative that already appears in the Options Considered
Over-engineering is judged against the design's own Goals/Non-Goals, and the verdict is Revise (no fabricated Blocker)

evals.expected_output: Recognize the design looks complete but ignores integration/data blind spots: CRM API rate limits, failure/retry/resume on a failed page, upstream deletions never removed by full reload, local-edit conflict policy, partial-sync consistency, and monitoring/alerting. Surface these as D5 findings framed as worth checking, and flag that Open Questions wrongly says 'None'. Verdict Revise.
expectations: The review surfaces multiple Blind Spot (D5) findings, including at least: Acme API rate limits, failure handling/retry beyond 'log and stop', and upstream deletions not being removed by a full reload
It flags that 'log the error and stops' leaves the contacts table in a partial/inconsistent state with no resume or retry
It notes the absence of conflict resolution for locally edited contacts and of monitoring/alerting on sync failure
It flags that Open Questions states 'None' when real unknowns remain (e.g., deletion and conflict policy) — a D3 conformance finding
Blind spots are framed as considerations to check rather than as certain defects, and findings cite concrete locations
